compiled_state.title: [fix-bug Step 4]: safeguard trigger misses enforcement additions -> autonomous safeguard-file edits slip past user approval
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview

Fix-bug Step 4 user-approval triggers for safeguard files fire on scope REDUCTION but not scope ADDITION. An autonomously-created ticket can propose adding new enforcement on a safeguard file, and fix-bug auto-approves and implements because the trigger phrase (reduces enforcement scope of any safety gate) does not match.

Concrete instance: ticket 5a5d-461b (no CLI_user tag, agent-authored) proposed adding a PreBash guard function. Fix-bug autonomously implemented it in commit cd4c7268 on 2026-04-15. A major behavioral change shipped without user review, and broke COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md Step 4.5 (tracked as 4344-7243).

Expected: fix-bug Step 4 requires user approval for ANY modification to files under plugins/dso/hooks/, hooks/lib/, pre-commit-*.sh, review-gate.sh, and PreToolUse dispatchers — regardless of whether the change expands or reduces enforcement. Current trigger scope is too narrow.

Impact: undetected behavioral drift when autonomous fix tracks modify safeguard surfaces. Difficult to catch because the trigger phrasing looks like it covers safeguard edits.

Related: ticket 4344-7243 (circular-dependency blocker caused by cd4c7268). This ticket captures the GOVERNANCE gap; 4344-7243 captures the specific breakage.

comments.body: Triaged (debug-everything Bug-Fix Mode): bug targets an LLM-behavioral skill/agent/prompt file. Per CLAUDE.md rule 22 and behavioral-testing-standard Rule 5, any modification requires dedicated bot-psychologist investigation and explicit user approval. DEFERRED: batch-escalating to end-of-session user review; no code change applied this session.
comments.author: Test
